step_by_step_reasoning: We trace the architecture starting from the Almosafer web/mobile users. They access the application through CloudFront, which uses Lambda@Edge for geo-routing decisions. Static assets are served from S3. Dynamic requests go through a pool of 60+ Elastic Load Balancers that split traffic: 20% to a legacy monolith running on EC2, and 80% to over 250 microservices running on EKS. The microservices store and retrieve data from a self-managed Mongo/Kafka cluster on EC2, and managed relational databases on RDS. Performance monitoring is managed through EC2 collectors reporting to CloudWatch.

graph.name: Seera Group: Scaling Almosafer to 300k Requests Per Minute with EKS
graph.notes: Seera Group migrated 80% of their legacy travel booking monolith to over 250 microservices (1400 pods) on Amazon EKS, handling peak traffic of 300,000 requests per minute during major travel events.

nodes.name: Almosafer Users
nodes.notes: DATA_PEEK: Handles ~300k requests per minute at peak.

nodes.name: CloudFront CDN
nodes.notes: Serves static content and acts as the entry point for API traffic.

nodes.name: Edge Routing Lambda
nodes.notes: Determines user region and injects geolocation headers for backend APIs.

nodes.name: Application Load Balancer
nodes.notes: WORKLOAD_PEEK: Over 60 LBs deployed to separate backend API and web traffic.

nodes.name: Legacy Monolith
nodes.notes: Receives remaining 20% of legacy application traffic.

nodes.name: Microservices EKS Cluster
nodes.notes: WORKLOAD_PEEK: Runs 250+ microservices across 1400+ pods in production.

nodes.name: Relational Databases
nodes.notes: Managed SQL databases for relational application storage.

nodes.name: MongoDB & Kafka Cluster
nodes.notes: Self-managed NoSQL data store and stream broker for microservices messaging.

nodes.name: Monitoring Agent
nodes.notes: Collects logs and operational metrics from the compute fleets.

nodes.name: CloudWatch
nodes.notes: Centralized dashboard for tracking performance and operational metrics.

edges.notes: User sends dynamic API / web request.
Route request to Load Balancing tier.
Route 20% of traffic to legacy monolith.
Fetch/Publish data to MongoDB or Kafka.
Aggregate and monitor via CloudWatch dashboards.
